original_hypothesis.title: DevFund reward distribution precision loss can be exploited by sending many small payments to divert most ETH to owner
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: DevFund.receive() computes amountPerWeight = msg.value / totalDevWeight. If msg.value < totalDevWeight, amountPerWeight becomes 0 and the full msg.value is forwarded to owner as 'remaining', starving devs. An adversary can repeatedly send tiny ETH amounts (or structure payments) to maximize rounding-to-zero, shifting incoming reward stream to owner and damaging dev payout integrity.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Although this does not directly enrich the attacker (unless they are owner), it is an operationally realistic griefing vector and a governance/incentive integrity issue. It can also occur naturally due to small protocol fees; quantify expected frequency on Base.
deployment_assumptions: DevFund is used as devAddress in NukeFund (deployTasks passes devFund as devAddress)
totalDevWeight is high enough that common inbound payments are frequently smaller than totalDevWeight (in wei)
Owner address is able to receive ETH
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: deployTasks.ts: deploy-nuke-fund args include devFund.getAddress() as devAddress; DevFund.sol receive() forwards 'remaining' (potentially all) to owner when amountPerWeight truncates to 0

proof_of_signal.pattern: amountPerWeight = msg.value / totalDevWeight; remaining = msg.value - (amountPerWeight * totalDevWeight); if amountPerWeight==0 => remaining==msg.value => sent to owner

preconditions: Inbound payments to DevFund are often small relative to totalDevWeight
Attacker can send arbitrary small ETH transfers to DevFund (permissionless)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Integer division truncation causes systematic skim to owner on small deposits
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Owner benefits; attacker can still grief by causing more deposits to be small
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time DevFund receives funds in small increments

operational_questions: What is the expected distribution of inbound payment sizes from NukeFund receive() devShare?
At current totalDevWeight, what fraction of payments will round to zero per-weight?
Can batching at source reduce loss, or should DevFund accumulate and distribute differently?
validation_requirements: Simulate NukeFund->DevFund devShare sizes across expected mint/trade/forge fees
Compute expected lost fraction due to truncation over time

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Determine totalDevWeight and minimum inbound payment size that yields amountPerWeight=0
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Send many dust payments below that threshold to route all ETH to owner
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Amplify by encouraging protocol fee flows to fragment (if any external integrations do so)

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Developer reward starvation; governance/incentive breakdown; reputational damage
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium; requires contract change or operational mitigation

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: High count of small inbound transfers to DevFund with no increase in totalRewardDebt (or minimal)
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Devs observe low/zero claimable rewards despite fund inflows
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Many dust sends from a small set of addresses

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
